text.title: Task Template Sections
text.summary: All sections of the TASK_NNN.md template v2.0 with purpose and constraints for each.

list.item: Title line: # TASK_XXX: {Title}
HTML comment: <!-- SUMMARY: one sentence business value --> (machine-parseable for dependency trees)
Metadata table: Complexity (simple/normal/complex), Priority (P0/P1/P2), Created date, Project, Feature

rule.statement: Write the SUMMARY comment in the header before any other section — it is the value this task delivers, used by subsequent tasks' dependency trees.
rule.rationale: The SUMMARY comment is parsed by planning agents to build dependency trees for downstream tasks. Missing or vague summaries ("implement stuff") break the context-passing chain that saves 30-40% of subsequent tasks' token budgets.

list.item: SDD References table: Document | Path | Sections — one row per relevant document
Dependency Tree: visual tree of completed tasks this one depends on, with summaries, files, patterns, key code
Links to completed TASK_NNN.md files: "Read Summary section before starting"
FR-X section: full text of each functional requirement this task implements
AD-X section: full text of each architectural decision this task applies
NFR-X section: if non-functional requirements apply to this task

rule.statement: Copy the full FR and AD text into the task file — do not reference by ID only; executor agents reading only the task file must not need to re-read spec and design.
rule.rationale: The task file must be self-contained within the 100k budget. "See FR-001 in spec.md" sends the executor back to load the full spec, wasting 10-15k tokens on re-reading. Inline the relevant sections only.

list.item: Description: 2-4 sentences of what to do + business value
Goals: 3 specific measurable goals (not "implement X" — "X function returns Y given Z")
Acceptance Criteria: Given/When/Then format, each marked with the FR-X it covers
Out of Scope: explicit exclusions written BEFORE Goals to prevent scope creep during execution
Files to Change: CREATE/MODIFY table with exact paths — maximum 8 files
Risks and Mitigations: concrete mitigations for each risk
Potential Blockers: checklist — treat as exit conditions, not decorative
Testing: unit test file paths + integration test descriptions
Subtasks: research → implement → test → verify (filled by planner)
Implementation: (filled by executor during execution — findings per subtask)
Summary: (filled after completion — what was done, key decisions, files changed, test results)
Lessons Learned: (optional — patterns and mistakes for .aidocs/memory/)

rule.statement: The Summary section must be filled by the executor after completion — it becomes the dependency tree entry for all downstream tasks.
rule.rationale: If Summary is empty, downstream tasks cannot build a dependency tree, breaking the context-passing chain. The Summary format (What was done / Key decisions / Files changed / Test results) is parseable by planning agents.
